question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $86,000 on November 28?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price History
evidences.content: ### Late 2024 On Nov. 7, 2024, Bitcoin's price hit another all-time high of $75,902 following Donald Trump's re-election, a 9% increase from the closing price on Nov. 5. On Nov. 10, 2024, Bitcoin reached another milestone, crossing the $80,000 threshold.1 On Nov. 11, it continued pushing boundaries, and two days later, it closed at $90,574.1 Market exuberance continued apace in the following weeks, pushing Bitcoin to remarkable highs of $99,817 on Oanda, $99,860 on Coinbase, and $99,852 on Gemini on Nov. 22, 2024, by midday.8 9 10 On Dec. 8, 2024, Bitcoin broke through $100,000 and closed at $101,237.1 [...] In 2020, the economy shut down due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Bitcoin's price burst once again. The cryptocurrency opened the year at $7,768.1 The pandemic shutdown and subsequent government policies fed investors' fears about the global economy and accelerated Bitcoin's rise. At the close on Nov. 23, Bitcoin was trading for $18,383. Its price closed at $27,081 on Dec. 28, 2020, increasing 248.62% from the start of that year.1 ### 2021 to 2023 Bitcoin took less than a month in 2021 to break its 2020 price record, surpassing $45,000 by Feb. 8, 2021. By mid-April, prices hit ne...

evidences.title: Will Bitcoin Hit $100K in 2026?
evidences.content: Is the old 4-year halving cycle still valid in 2026? It’s becoming less reliable. The halving still reduces new supply, but Bitcoin now reacts more to macro liquidity, ETF flows, and institutional behavior than to the traditional cycle timing. Should I buy Bitcoin right now? That depends on your risk tolerance and time horizon. Bitcoin looks attractive for long-term holders who can handle volatility, but near-term risks remain. What is the most likely price range for Bitcoin at the end of 2026? The base case among analysts sits between $95,000 and $120,000. The bullish scenario reaches $150,000-$220,000, while the bearish scenario keeps it between $60,000 and $85,000. ### Related Articles What Is Venice Token? Why This Underrated AI Coin Is Skyrocketing Altcoin News [...] The path exists. Strong ETF inflows, corporate buying, improving liquidity, and the post-halving supply squeeze can push Bitcoin well above $100K. Many top analysts — Tom Lee, Cathie Wood, JPMorgan, and others — still see this as a realistic outcome if macro conditions improve. At the same time, the risks are real and significant. Geopolitical tensions (especially the US-Israel-Iran situation), sticky inflation...

evidences.title: What Is the IBIT Spot Bitcoin ETF by BlackRock?
evidences.content: These ETFs may experience additional volatility due to basis risk and leverage used in futures contracts. IBIT, in contrast, is a spot ETF that holds actual Bitcoin, providing more direct exposure to price movements. ### Other Spot Bitcoin ETFs While all spot Bitcoin ETFs track Bitcoin’s performance, they differ in fees and management. IBIT, managed by BlackRock, currently has an expense ratio of 0.25%. Comparatively, Grayscale’s Bitcoin Trust charges 1.5%, while Franklin Templeton’s Digital Holding Trust bills 0.19%, with the latter offering the lowest fees of all spot Bitcoin ETFs (at the time of writing). ## Potential Impact of IBIT on the Bitcoin Market ### Increased Institutional Adoption [...] However, it still has typically low correlation with traditional assets like stocks and bonds; hence, long time horizons can provide a modest diversifying effect in a portfolio. To navigate volatility, consider using common portfolio management methods like Dollar Cost Averaging and regular rebalancing. ## Conclusion BlackRock’s entry into the Bitcoin market with IBIT has had a significant impact on the cryptocurrency landscape, bridging the gap between traditional finance and digita...

evidences.title: What Is IBIT? A Guide to BlackRock's Spot Bitcoin ETF
evidences.content: ### What Is IBIT (iShares Bitcoin Trust)? IBIT is a spot Bitcoin ETF operated by BlackRock that tracks Bitcoin's price. It allows investors to tap into the financial opportunities of the Bitcoin market without directly holding the asset. After being approved by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in January 2024, IBIT began trading on the NASDAQ stock exchange, allowing people to invest in Bitcoin without setting up a separate crypto wallet to store BTC. IBIT at a glance: What it Holds: Bitcoin Fund Manager: BlackRock Custodian: Coinbase Custody Trust Company holds the Bitcoin securely Annual Fee: 0.25% of your asset's value Approved By: The U.S. SEC on January 11, 2024 [...] This allows traditional investors to gain exposure to Bitcoin’s price movements through their chosen brokers without needing to buy and hold BTC directly in a digital wallet. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) approved IBIT and 10 other spot Bitcoin ETF applications in January 2024. Since then, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have grown to collectively hold over 7% of Bitcoin's total supply. ### Spot ETFs vs. Futures-based Bitcoin ETFS Bitcoin ETFs can be categorized as either spot ETFs...

evidences.title: What is BlackRock's IBIT spot bitcoin ETF? - Coinbase
evidences.content: ETFs are traded on traditional stock exchanges, which typically offer higher liquidity than digital asset platforms. This means that users can trade shares of the ETF at prevailing market prices without significantly impacting the market. Spot bitcoin ETFs are subject to regulatory oversight, providing users with a sense of protection that may not be present when dealing with unregulated digital asset platforms. ## What is BlackRock's IBIT? BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) is a spot bitcoin ETF that was approved by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on January 11, 2024. Users can participate in IBIT through existing brokerage accounts on the NASDAQ stock exchange. [...] BlackRock, the issuer of IBIT, is a global financial management corporation. ## What is a Spot Bitcoin ETF? A spot bitcoinETF is a type of financial product that holds actual bitcoin as its underlying asset. This financial product allows users to gain exposure to bitcoin's price movements without the need to acquire and store the cryptocurrency themselves. This can be particularly beneficial for those who find the process of acquiring and storing bitcoin complex or risky. [...] Nederlands N...

evidences.title: IBIT ETF: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust Explained
evidences.content: ## Conclusion The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) represents one of the most important bridges between traditional financial markets and the Bitcoin ecosystem. By holding Bitcoin directly while trading as a conventional exchange-traded fund, IBIT allows investors to access Bitcoin exposure through familiar brokerage infrastructure. With its large Bitcoin reserves, strong trading liquidity, and backing from the world’s largest asset manager, IBIT has quickly established itself as a central vehicle for institutional participation in the spot Bitcoin ETF market. As the digital asset investment landscape continues to evolve, funds like IBIT are likely to remain key entry points for investors seeking regulated exposure to Bitcoin. [...] Just days later, on January 10, 2024, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission approved the first group of spot Bitcoin ETFs, marking a historic shift in how digital assets could be accessed through traditional financial markets. Unlike earlier Bitcoin futures ETFs that track derivatives contracts, IBIT directly purchases and stores Bitcoin within the trust. Shares of the ETF represent proportional claims on the Bitcoin reserves held by the fund....

evidences.title: iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) | Spot Bitcoin ETP
evidences.content: The iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF's use of derivatives may decrease its returns, increase volatility, and expose it to additional operational and counterparty risks—that is, the risk that another party in a transaction may fail to meet their contractual obligations. Losses may arise from derivative holdings due to limited liquidity in secondary markets and unexpected market changes. Writing covered call options on IBIT shares limits the Trust's gains above the option exercise price. The Trust remains exposed to losses below the exercise price, as premiums may not cover declines tied to bitcoin or IBIT volatility. Additionally, the Trust could be impacted by declining share values, restrictions on trading options, and other risks linked to options strategies. [...] This material is provided for educational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. The information contained herein is based on current tax laws, which may change in the future. BlackRock cannot be held responsible for any direct or incidental loss resulting from applying any of the information provided in this publication or from any other source mentioned. The information provided in this materia...

evidences.title: MicroStrategy Bitcoin Acquisition: Strategic $168M ...
evidences.content: ### Expert Analysis and Market Reception Financial analysts generally recognize MicroStrategy’s strategy as unique rather than indicative of broad corporate trends. Some institutional investors appreciate the pure-play Bitcoin exposure through traditional equity markets, while others remain skeptical about concentration risk. Credit rating agencies monitor the company’s debt levels relative to its volatile asset base. Meanwhile, cryptocurrency advocates often cite MicroStrategy as evidence of Bitcoin’s maturation as an institutional asset class. The company’s substantial holdings could potentially influence Bitcoin market dynamics during large transactions, though the firm typically uses over-the-counter desks to minimize market impact. [...] ## Corporate Bitcoin Adoption: Context and Market Impact MicroStrategy’s actions exist within a broader context of increasing institutional cryptocurrency adoption. Several publicly traded companies now hold Bitcoin on their balance sheets, though none approach MicroStrategy’s scale. This trend began gaining momentum in 2020 and has evolved through various market cycles. The company’s consistent buying, even during price corrections, signal...

evidences.title: API Client to Binance
evidences.content: binancer 1.2.0 Reference # binancer An R client to the Public Rest API for Binance. API docs: Quick intro to using `binancer` by getting the most recent ~USD price of the cryptocurrencies supported on Binance: ``` library(binancer) library(binancer)binance_coins_prices() binance_coins_prices() ``` Getting data on a specific symbol pair, e.g. the most recent Bitcoin/USDT changes: ``` (klines <- binance_klines('BTCUSDT', interval = '1m'))(klines<- binance_klines('BTCUSDT' = '1m')) ``` Visualize this data, e.g. on a simple line chart: ``` library(ggplot2) library(ggplot2)ggplot(klines, aes(close_time, close)) + geom_line() ggplot(klines aes(close_time close)) + geom_line() ``` Poor man’s candle chart with some `ggplot2` tweaks: [...] library(data.table) library(data.table)klines <- rbindlist(lapply(klines<- rbindlist(lapply( c('ETHBTC', 'ARKBTC', 'NEOBTC', 'IOTABTC'), c('ETHBTC' 'ARKBTC' 'NEOBTC' 'IOTABTC') binance_klines, binance_klines interval = '15m', = '15m' limit = 424)) = 4 24))ggplot(klines, aes(open_time)) + ggplot(klines aes(open_time)) + geom_linerange(aes(ymin = open, ymax = close, color = close < open), size = 2) + geom_linerange(aes(= open = close = close< open) = 2)...

While another Fed rate cut might not arrive until later in the first quarter of 2026, expectations for balance sheet adjustments or other easing measures and "liquidity injections" could help rebuild optimism around risk assets including BTC, she said. ### BTC headed to $84K, Ledn CIO says Meanwhile, technical indicators suggest bitcoin may still have plenty of room to fall, said John Glover, chief investment officer at crypto lending firm Ledn. He noted that to a breakdown below the 23.6% Fibonacci retracement level at just below $100,000 opened the path to the next key support level, sitting at around $84,000. Image 2: Analyst John Glover outlines bitcoin's bear market trajectory (Ledn/TradingView) [...] Analyst John Glover outlines bitcoin's bear market trajectory (Ledn/TradingView) Glover believes the current pullback is part of bitcoin's bear market, forecasting volatile action for the upcoming months. "We’ll likely see prices back above $100,000 before any sustained break below $90,000," he said, noting that the full correction could play out through the summer of 2026. evidences.title: Why (oh why) did Bitcoin crash just in time for Thanksgiving?
evidences.content: The SEC finally got round to calling them that too, and on 16 November it announced $250,000 in civil penalties against two ICOs, Paragon and CarrierEQ, and told them to notify investors they are eligible for refunds if they still own the token or sold it at a loss. Here’s the kicker: the buyer compensation had to be at the original price and paid in fiat currency. None of your Bitcoin/Tether nonsense here. That’s a cool $27m that those two companies needed to find pretty fast. Now look at the chart below. The spikes are sales: red means selling below the predominant price, and green means buying above the predominant price. Consider where the price drop is, two days before the SEC announcement. 7,000BTC are sold at a price of about $6,000: that’s $42m. [...] Sitemap Open in app Sign up Sign in did Bitcoin crash just in time for Thanksgiving? Image 3: Charles Arthur Charles Arthur Follow 13 min read · Nov 22, 2018 would have done well. At first. $8,000 turned into, wow, $20,000! And if you were able to get out by mid-May, you’d have been OK. Since then, ehh, not so much. The price noodled around $6,000 for months. (I do hope that the Washington Post will revist this group in Ken...
